text:

          Graphite
          Watercolour
          Others
        
      
      About Me
    
  





This is my favourite media. I love its simplicity, just a piece of paper and a few graphite pencils. And an eraser. And a blending stump… Anyway, less stuff than watercolour or other media.
You only have to focus on greyscales, not on colours, which I think makes it easier and more enjoyable for newbies like me.

title: Cityscapes Gallery
text:
Home
      
        Photography
        
          Photography Home
          Animals
          Landscapes
          Cityscapes
          Things
          Anaglyph
        
      
      
        Drawings
        
          Drawings Home
          Graphite
          Watercolour
          Others
        
      
      About Me
    
  





I always find it challenging to take decent pictures of in cities. First of all, there are just way too many humans. I don’t like to take pictures of humans, except for my own kids. Then usually I am out and about in cities during daytime, when the light is way too harsh.
The silver lining is that the combination of the over-population and harsh light really forces you to look for a composition. Geometrical shapes, light and shadow contrasts, black and white. You cannot just rely on Nature built-in wow factor.





No images found for this category.

title: Anaglyph Gallery
text:
Home

        Drawings
        
          Drawings Home
          Graphite
          Watercolour
          Others
        
      
      About Me
    
  





If you are old enough (at least old as me that is), you might remember the 3D anaglyph effect. This was the prototype of much sophisticated 3D effects that came years later. This one is based, in simple terms, on shifting the red and the blue channels a bit (read a more in depth description here).
To enjoy this gallery you will need a pair of those glasses with the two colour lenses.

I have always loved animals. Since getting a camera, I have taken countless pictures of every creature I come across. I must have thousands of squirrel photos on my hard drive alone…
It is always hard to try to keep this gallery to a reasonable size, discarding images that might be fun, but technically not that good. Here is my “best of” selection.

title: About Me
text:
Home
      
        Photography
        
          Photography Home
          Animals
          Landscapes
          Cityscapes
          Things
          Anaglyph
        
      
      
        Drawings
        
          Drawings Home
          Graphite
          Watercolour
          Others
        
      
      About Me
    
  






I’m an amateur photographer, or more accurately someone who enjoys experimenting with a camera while spending time outdoors. Photography, for me, is less about chasing perfection and more about paying attention: to light, to small details, and to the everyday environments I might otherwise overlook. It gives structure to my time outside and turns ordinary moments into something worth observing more closely.

My work focuses mainly on nature: landscapes, animals, and quiet scenes that do not usually make headlines. There are no extreme expeditions or rare, far-flung subjects here. Most images come from familiar places and common encounters, with the occasional visit to a zoo for subjects I would not otherwise see. This is not a collection of technically perfect or award-winning photographs taken with high-end gear, but rather a body of work I am genuinely proud of, images that reflect patience, curiosity, and the simple effort of showing up and trying.


Back to Home

      About Me
    
  





Besides animals, being outside is something else I love. Bonus point for being away from civilization and near water.
It does not have to be an exotic locations reached after hours of travelling, hiking, and night after night of camping. Most of these pictures were taken in local parks or 10 meters from the door of a cottage.
